title: Creating cookies without the secure flag is security-sensitive
description: Creating cookies without the "secure" flag is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity HotspotcweprivacyWhen a cookie is protected with thesecureattribute set totrueit will not be send by the browser over an unencrypted HTTP request and thus cannot be observed by an unauthorized person during a man-in-the-middle attack.Ask Yourself Whetherthe cookie is for instance asession-cookienot designed to be sent over non-HTTPS communication.it’s not sure that the website containsmixed contentor not (ie HTTPS everywhere or not)There is a risk if you answered yes to any of those questions.Recommended Secure Coding PracticesIt is recommended to useHTTPseverywhere so setting thesecureflag totrueshould be the default behaviour when creating cookies.Set thesecureflag tot
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Cookie creation (HttpCookie, CookieOptions) without Secure = true OR Secure explicitly set to false
logic.triggering_conditions: (new HttpCookie OR new CookieOptions OR Cookies.Append) AND NOT (Secure = true) AND NOT test code
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude cookies that have Secure = true, test code, localhost/development environments
logic.reasoning: Cookies without the Secure flag can be transmitted over unencrypted HTTP connections, making them vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks. The rule detects HttpCookie and CookieOptions instantiation without Secure = true. It looks for patterns where cookies are created but don't contain 'Secure = true' or explicitly set 'Secure = false'.

should_trigger: var cookie = new HttpCookie("SessionId") { Secure = false };
should_not_trigger: var cookie = new HttpCookie("SessionId") { Secure = true };
new CookieOptions { Secure = true, HttpOnly = true }
